content: Self-Refine’s task-agnostic iterative self-improvement framework can be repurposed as a backbone to unify fragmented LLM self-reflection mechanisms by grounding multi-perspective reflection in repeated cycles of self-feedback, eliminating the need for custom structural overhauls for each new reflection perspective. This unification creates a modular reflection system that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no additional training or labeled data, while enabling multi-perspective inconsistency detection that existing self-reflection mechanisms focus on.
